ddls:
CREATE TABLE "videos" (
  "video_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each video */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the video */,
  "upload_date" TEXT /* Date the video was uploaded */,
  "duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the video in seconds */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the video (e.g., completed, encoding) */,
  "file_size" INTEGER /* Size of the original video file in bytes */,
  "resolution" TEXT /* Resolution of the original video (e.g., 1080p, 720p) */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the video (e.g., documentary, tutorial) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the video content */,
  "thumbnail_url" TEXT /* URL of the video thumbnail image */,
  "tags" TEXT /* Comma-separated list of tags for the video */,
  "view_count" INTEGER /* Number of times the video has been viewed */,
  "like_count" INTEGER /* Number of likes the video has received */,
  "dislike_count" INTEGER /* Number of dislikes the video has received */,
  "comment_count" INTEGER /* Number of comments on the video */,
  "last_modified" TEXT /* Date and time of the last modification */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("video_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, editor, viewer) */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the user */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the user account was created */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Whether the user account is active */,
  "access_token" TEXT /* Access token for API authentication */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "video_formats" (
  "format_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each format */,
  "format_name" TEXT /* Name of the format (e.g., VP9, H.264) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the format */,
  "is_supported" INTEGER /* Whether the format is currently supported by the system */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date and time the format information was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("format_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "bitrates" (
  "bitrate_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each bitrate */,
  "bitrate_value" TEXT /* Value of the bitrate (e.g., 339 kbps) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the bitrate usage */,
  "is_supported" INTEGER /* Whether the bitrate is currently supported by the system */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date and time the bitrate information was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("bitrate_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "statuses" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each status */,
  "status_name" TEXT /* Name of the status (e.g., completed, encoding) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the status */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Whether the status is currently active */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date and time the status information was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "encoding_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "encoding_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the encoding task */,
  "log_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of the log entry */,
  "message" TEXT /* Message detailing the outcome of the encoding attempt */,
  "log_level" TEXT /* Level of the log entry (e.g., info, warning, error) */,
  "log_source" TEXT /* Source of the log entry (e.g., encoder, system) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "download_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "download_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the download request */,
  "log_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of the log entry */,
  "message" TEXT /* Message detailing the outcome of the download request */,
  "log_level" TEXT /* Level of the log entry (e.g., info, warning, error) */,
  "log_source" TEXT /* Source of the log entry (e.g., downloader, system) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id")
)
